title: Nellaiappar Temple
tagline: Nellaiappar Temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: The Nellaiappar Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva, located in Tirunelveli, a city in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu.
overview.description: The Nellaiappar Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva, located in Tirunelveli, a city in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Shiva is worshipped as Nellaiappar (also called Venuvananathar) represented by the lingam and his consort Parvati is depicted as Kanthimathi Amman. The deity Vishnu is also worshipped here, having witnessed their wedding according to legend. Hence, this temple is regarded as an abhimana kshetram of Vaishnavism.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Pettai, Tirunelveli
topPlaces.description: Pettai is an industrial suburban area in Tirunelveli. It is approximately 6 kilometers of downtown Tirunelveli along the Southern Railway line to Tenkasi.

topPlaces.name: Melapalayam
topPlaces.description: Melapalayam is a neighborhood of Tirunelveli City in Tamil Nadu, India situated on the east bank of the Thamirabarani River. The neighborhood's name comes from its location west (Tamil: mela) of Palayamkottai.

topPlaces.name: Uchchhishta Ganapathy Temple
topPlaces.description: Uchchhishta Ganapathy Temple is an Uchchhishta Ganapati temple situated in Tirunelveli in the State of Tamil Nadu, in India. It is said to be the biggest Vinayagar temple in Asia.

topPlaces.name: Holy Trinity Cathedral, Palayamkottai
topPlaces.description: Holy Trinity Cathedral is the cathedral church of Tirunelveli Diocese under Church of South India. Marvellous Church Crafted with the help of God.

topPlaces.name: Mela Thiruvenkatanathapuram
topPlaces.description: Mela Thiruvenkatanathapuram also known as Thirunankovil is a hamlet situated on the bank of river Thamirabarani. It is about 10 km from Tirunelveli, a district headquarters in Tamil Nadu, South India.

topPlaces.name: Palayamkottai taluk
topPlaces.description: Palayamkottai taluk is a taluk of Tirunelveli district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The headquarters is the town of Palayamkottai.

topPlaces.name: Palayamkottai
topPlaces.description: Palayamkottai (also Palayankottai) is a neighbourhood in Tirunelveli City, incorporated within the Tirunelveli City Municipal Corporation. It is situated on the east bank of the Thamirabarani river, with the exception of its downtown area, which is present on the west bank.

topPlaces.name: Palayamkottai Central Prison
topPlaces.description: Palayamkottai Central Prison is located in Palayamkottai, Tirunelveli District, India. The prison was built during 1880 and was operating as a district jail until 1929.

items.note: Pettai is an industrial suburban area in Tirunelveli. It is approximately 6 kilometers of downtown Tirunelveli along the Southern Railway line to Tenkasi.

Palayamkottai taluk is a taluk of Tirunelveli district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The headquarters is the town of Palayamkottai.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Nellaiappar Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Nellaiappar Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Nellaiappar Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (89 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (85 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Nellaiappar Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Nellaiappar Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Nellaiappar Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Nellaiappar Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Nellaiappar Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Pettai, Tirunelveli, Melapalayam, Uchchhishta Ganapathy Temple, Holy Trinity Cathedral, Palayamkottai, Mela Thiruvenkatanathapuram.
